question.text: In the Sunita Gupta case, what was the nature of the appellant's defense regarding her trading activity?

answer.text: She claimed she was trading as a sub-broker on behalf of her clients and had no connection with the Vishvas group
She claimed she was not aware of the PFUTP Regulations
She claimed the trades were executed without her authorization
She claimed all trades were executed by a computer algorithm

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Sunita Gupta's defense had two main planks: (1) she was trading as a sub-broker on behalf of clients (especially Perfect Car Scanners Pvt. Ltd.), and (2) she had no connection with the Vishvas group and synchronization happened by chance. **The Trap:** Her sub-broker defense was rejected because all trades were in her own UCC, her broker Parasram confirmed she traded as a client, and she produced no evidence of client relationships. **Cross-Exam Context:** The distinction between client and sub-broker capacity is tested in NISM Series III-A. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always maintain proper documentation of your capacity (client vs. sub-broker) and client relationships - verbal claims without documentation will not stand.
